_comment: Grounded talking points, keyed by sourceSlug -> matchSlug -> [points]. Merged into lib/twin_matches.json by scripts/build-twin-matches.mjs. The (costly) grounded automation writes here so re-running the generator never wipes them. Every point must be traceable to the attendee's real scraped LinkedIn data.

bartanian: You're both founder/CEO owner-operators building OOH/DOOH assets — Guillermo leading ALOOH across LATAM, Kevin running KEVANI's full-motion digital and premium static inventory in California, New York and Maryland. A natural exchange on how asset development differs market-to-market.

Guillermo writes about DOOH digitalization, new formats and technology on urban surfaces; Kevin's feed is exactly that — launching landmarks like Nexus, The Trio and Squareview in Times Square. Compare notes on building iconic digital media destinations.
You both post about AI applied to OOH — Guillermo on technology applied to OOH, Kevin specifically on using AI to localize out-of-home creative at scale. A concrete topic to dig into.
efthimios-sotos-a5468722: Efthimios is COO/CFO of KEVANI, a US DOOH media owner, while Guillermo leads the LATAM OOH ecosystem at ALOOH — a complementary pairing of operational/financial leadership with regional industry-building. Good ground to compare what scaling an OOH business looks like in each market.

You both promote large-format, high-impact iconic media — Guillermo on new OOH formats for LATAM cities, Efthimios posting about KEVANI assets like Melrose Lights West and '12,000 square feet of high-visibility, high-impact digital media'. Easy opener on premium digital inventory.
Efthimios shares industry data points (an OAAA/Harris Poll, Talon OOH benchmarks); Guillermo writes about measurement and data use in OOH — a shared interest in the numbers behind the medium.
markboidman: Guillermo sits on the World Out of Home Organization board; Mark served on the board of the Digital Signage Federation and is the OOH/DOOH-dedicated investment banker at Solomon Partners. Two industry-body veterans of out-of-home — a strong opener on where the global OOH industry is heading.

Mark is the author of 'Times Square Everywhere' and posts that 'it's time for DOOH to move beyond the billboard'; Guillermo writes about DOOH evolution and new formats for LATAM. A direct conversation on the next wave of DOOH.

Mark advises OOH operators globally (Clear Channel, OUTFRONT and others) and runs Solomon's Global Media Summit; Guillermo leads OOH integration across LATAM. Complementary angle: regional industry-building meets cross-border OOH M&A and capital.
